chief_complaint: A 44-year-old male presents with persistent symptoms following SARS-CoV-2 infection, including severe cognitive and motor fatigue, word-finding difficulties, and impaired concentration.
history: The patient has a history of post-COVID-19 syndrome (PCS) with symptoms persisting for an unknown duration. He reports no significant past medical history, and his medication list is unknown. Social history is unremarkable.
exam: Vital signs are within normal limits. Physical examination reveals no abnormalities.
labs: Serum analysis demonstrates anti-VGLUT2 autoantibodies. Neuropsychological testing reveals marked deficits in alertness, attention, fluency, and processing speed. IVIG therapy yields subjective but no objective improvement.
correct_diagnosis: Post-COVID-19 syndrome associated with anti-VGLUT2 autoantibodies
differentials: Post-COVID-19 syndrome associated with anti-VGLUT2 autoantibodies
correct_ranking: Post-COVID-19 syndrome associated with anti-VGLUT2 autoantibodies
